day1_insight: There's been no measurable rain lately, so there's no runoff driving contamination, and strong summer sunlight is helping disinfect the water. The model's main signals right now: hours since high tide is lowering risk — the timing relative to high tide affects how concentrated bacteria are at the shoreline; the lack of recent rain is keeping runoff-driven contamination low; and the lack of recent rain is keeping runoff-driven contamination low. The last lab sample (5 MPN/100mL) is now 29 days old — beaches are sampled about weekly, so a reading this stale carries little weight, and this estimate leans on current conditions. It's a naturally clean, well-flushed open-coast beach (unsafe only ~3% of the time), so outside of storms it's reliably safe.
